forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,52 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: b180
+title: 'validators: swift casing — accept both Id and ID spellings, never flag conversions between them'
+---
+## What
+
+Update the Swift casing rule in `builtin/validators/swift/rules/casing.md` so the `Id`/`ID` spelling pair is explicitly bidirectionally accepted and never a finding. Three coordinated edits in that file plus one loader-level regression test:
+
+- [ ] **Replace the line-12 bullet** (`- \`ID\` and \`IDs\` are acronyms and follow this rule. DON'T: \`entryId\`, \`generatedTokenIds\`, \`schemaJson\`. DO: \`entryID\`, \`generatedTokenIDs\`, \`schemaJSON\` (matching Apple's own \`entryID:\`/\`objectID\` API labels).`) with this text, verbatim:
+
+  ```
+  - **`ID`/`Id` (and `IDs`/`Ids`) are BOTH accepted — never flag one toward the other.**
+    `entryID` and `entryId`, `generatedTokenIDs` and `generatedTokenIds` are all
+    valid; so are the leading forms `id`/`idToken`. Do not raise a finding whose
+    only substance is converting between the `Id` and `ID` spellings, in either
+    direction, on any declaration — new or pre-existing, public or private.
+    Renaming between these two forms is always churn: it touches every call
+    site, widens fork-merge surface, and changes no behavior. A finding that
+    proposes such a rename is a validator error.
+    - Within a SINGLE declaration's own name, don't mix spellings of the same
+      term (`tokenIdToEntryIDMap` is flaggable — pick one spelling inside one
+      name). Consistency across different declarations, files, or with
+      surrounding code is NOT required and NOT flaggable.
+    - This carve-out covers only the `Id`/`ID` pair. Other acronyms (`Url`,
+      `Json`, `Http`, `Eos`, `Uuid`, …) remain governed by the uniform-casing
+      rule above.
+  ```
+
+- [ ] **Reconcile line 10** (the uniform-acronym bullet): its wrong-examples list reads `` (`Url`, `Json`, `Http`, `deviceId` are all wrong) `` — remove `deviceId` from the list (it is now an accepted spelling per the carve-out), leaving the other examples intact.
+- [ ] **Reconcile line 13** (the "wins over local file prevalence" tiebreaker): its example is the ID pair (`entryId` → flag toward `entryID`), which now contradicts the carve-out. Rewrite the example to a non-ID acronym (`deviceUrl` → flag toward `deviceURL`) and keep the tiebreaker's substance; the new bullet's Id/ID carve-out takes precedence over it.
+- [ ] **Add a content regression test** alongside the existing builtin-loading tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/builtin/mod.rs` (pattern: `test_test_integrity_homes_no_hard_code`, ~line 231): load builtins via `load_builtins` and assert the swift `casing` ruleset's loaded rule text (a) contains `BOTH accepted`, and (b) no longer contains the retired directive text `DON'T: \`entryId\`` nor `deviceId` in the acronym wrong-examples — so the carve-out can't be silently reverted or half-applied.
+
+**Deploy note (not part of this card's code change):** `sah init` never deletes or refreshes already-deployed validator stores in consuming projects (known fossil behavior) — after this merges, deployed copies (e.g. in the mlx repo) need a redeploy/prune for the new rule to take effect there.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `builtin/validators/swift/rules/casing.md` contains the replacement bullet verbatim, and no longer contains `DON'T: \`entryId\`` anywhere.
+- [ ] Neither line 10's wrong-examples nor line 13's tiebreaker example references the `Id`/`ID` pair; the tiebreaker rule itself survives with a non-ID example.
+- [ ] The swift `casing` ruleset still loads cleanly through `load_builtins` (frontmatter intact — `name: casing` with its description).
+- [ ] The new regression test fails against the pre-change file content and passes after.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] New test in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/builtin/mod.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` asserting the loaded swift casing rule text contains `BOTH accepted` and contains neither `DON'T: \`entryId\`` nor a `deviceId` wrong-example.
+- [ ] Existing builtin loader tests (`test_focused_validators_have_clean_manifest_frontmatter` etc.) still pass — frontmatter untouched.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-validators builtin` — green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file
reverse_patch:

+++ modified
@@ -1,52 +0,0 @@

-title: 'validators: swift casing — accept both Id and ID spellings, never flag conversions between them'
----
-## What
-
-Update the Swift casing rule in `builtin/validators/swift/rules/casing.md` so the `Id`/`ID` spelling pair is explicitly bidirectionally accepted and never a finding. Three coordinated edits in that file plus one loader-level regression test:
-
-- [ ] **Replace the line-12 bullet** (`- \`ID\` and \`IDs\` are acronyms and follow this rule. DON'T: \`entryId\`, \`generatedTokenIds\`, \`schemaJson\`. DO: \`entryID\`, \`generatedTokenIDs\`, \`schemaJSON\` (matching Apple's own \`entryID:\`/\`objectID\` API labels).`) with this text, verbatim:
-
-  ```
-  - **`ID`/`Id` (and `IDs`/`Ids`) are BOTH accepted — never flag one toward the other.**
-    `entryID` and `entryId`, `generatedTokenIDs` and `generatedTokenIds` are all
-    valid; so are the leading forms `id`/`idToken`. Do not raise a finding whose
-    only substance is converting between the `Id` and `ID` spellings, in either
-    direction, on any declaration — new or pre-existing, public or private.
-    Renaming between these two forms is always churn: it touches every call
-    site, widens fork-merge surface, and changes no behavior. A finding that
-    proposes such a rename is a validator error.
-    - Within a SINGLE declaration's own name, don't mix spellings of the same
-      term (`tokenIdToEntryIDMap` is flaggable — pick one spelling inside one
-      name). Consistency across different declarations, files, or with
-      surrounding code is NOT required and NOT flaggable.
-    - This carve-out covers only the `Id`/`ID` pair. Other acronyms (`Url`,
-      `Json`, `Http`, `Eos`, `Uuid`, …) remain governed by the uniform-casing
-      rule above.
-  ```
-
-- [ ] **Reconcile line 10** (the uniform-acronym bullet): its wrong-examples list reads `` (`Url`, `Json`, `Http`, `deviceId` are all wrong) `` — remove `deviceId` from the list (it is now an accepted spelling per the carve-out), leaving the other examples intact.
-- [ ] **Reconcile line 13** (the "wins over local file prevalence" tiebreaker): its example is the ID pair (`entryId` → flag toward `entryID`), which now contradicts the carve-out. Rewrite the example to a non-ID acronym (`deviceUrl` → flag toward `deviceURL`) and keep the tiebreaker's substance; the new bullet's Id/ID carve-out takes precedence over it.
-- [ ] **Add a content regression test** alongside the existing builtin-loading tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/builtin/mod.rs` (pattern: `test_test_integrity_homes_no_hard_code`, ~line 231): load builtins via `load_builtins` and assert the swift `casing` ruleset's loaded rule text (a) contains `BOTH accepted`, and (b) no longer contains the retired directive text `DON'T: \`entryId\`` nor `deviceId` in the acronym wrong-examples — so the carve-out can't be silently reverted or half-applied.
-
-**Deploy note (not part of this card's code change):** `sah init` never deletes or refreshes already-deployed validator stores in consuming projects (known fossil behavior) — after this merges, deployed copies (e.g. in the mlx repo) need a redeploy/prune for the new rule to take effect there.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-
-- [ ] `builtin/validators/swift/rules/casing.md` contains the replacement bullet verbatim, and no longer contains `DON'T: \`entryId\`` anywhere.
-- [ ] Neither line 10's wrong-examples nor line 13's tiebreaker example references the `Id`/`ID` pair; the tiebreaker rule itself survives with a non-ID example.
-- [ ] The swift `casing` ruleset still loads cleanly through `load_builtins` (frontmatter intact — `name: casing` with its description).
-- [ ] The new regression test fails against the pre-change file content and passes after.
-
-## Tests
-
-- [ ] New test in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/builtin/mod.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` asserting the loaded swift casing rule text contains `BOTH accepted` and contains neither `DON'T: \`entryId\`` nor a `deviceId` wrong-example.
-- [ ] Existing builtin loader tests (`test_focused_validators_have_clean_manifest_frontmatter` etc.) still pass — frontmatter untouched.
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-validators builtin` — green.
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -3,48 +3,51 @@
 - claude-code
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: b180
-title: 'validators: swift casing — accept both Id and ID spellings, never flag conversions between them'
+title: 'validators: swift casing — accept both acronym spellings (URL/Url, ID/Id), never flag conversions between them'
 ---
 ## What

-Update the Swift casing rule in `builtin/validators/swift/rules/casing.md` so the `Id`/`ID` spelling pair is explicitly bidirectionally accepted and never a finding. Three coordinated edits in that file plus one loader-level regression test:
+Relax the Swift acronym-casing rule in `builtin/validators/swift/rules/casing.md`: both the uniform spelling (`URL`, `ID`, `JSON`) and the capitalized-word spelling (`Url`, `Id`, `Json`) are accepted in every position, and converting between them is never a finding. Rationale (user): the uniform-only rule is too picky to apply to other people's open source — spelling-conversion renames touch every call site, widen fork-merge surface, and change no behavior.

-- [ ] **Replace the line-12 bullet** (`- \`ID\` and \`IDs\` are acronyms and follow this rule. DON'T: \`entryId\`, \`generatedTokenIds\`, \`schemaJson\`. DO: \`entryID\`, \`generatedTokenIDs\`, \`schemaJSON\` (matching Apple's own \`entryID:\`/\`objectID\` API labels).`) with this text, verbatim:
+Edits to `builtin/validators/swift/rules/casing.md` (current line numbers):

+- [ ] **Rewrite the line-10 uniform-acronym bullet** (currently: "Acronyms and initialisms are cased uniformly … (`Url`, `Json`, `Http`, `deviceId` are all wrong)") and **delete the line-12 ID bullet and line-13 prevalence-tiebreaker bullet**, replacing all three with one flexible rule to this effect (wording may be polished, substance verbatim):
+
   ```
-  - **`ID`/`Id` (and `IDs`/`Ids`) are BOTH accepted — never flag one toward the other.**
-    `entryID` and `entryId`, `generatedTokenIDs` and `generatedTokenIds` are all
-    valid; so are the leading forms `id`/`idToken`. Do not raise a finding whose
-    only substance is converting between the `Id` and `ID` spellings, in either
-    direction, on any declaration — new or pre-existing, public or private.
-    Renaming between these two forms is always churn: it touches every call
-    site, widens fork-merge surface, and changes no behavior. A finding that
-    proposes such a rename is a validator error.
+  - **Acronym spelling is flexible — the uniform form (`URL`, `ID`, `JSON`, `HTTP`)
+    and the capitalized-word form (`Url`, `Id`, `Json`, `Http`) are BOTH accepted —
+    never flag one toward the other.** `entryID` and `entryId`, `baseURL` and
+    `baseUrl`, `schemaJSON` and `schemaJson` are all valid; so are the leading
+    lower forms `id`/`idToken`/`urlString`. Do not raise a finding whose only
+    substance is converting between the two spellings of the same acronym, in
+    either direction, on any declaration — new or pre-existing, public or
+    private. Such a rename is always churn: it touches every call site, widens
+    fork-merge surface, and changes no behavior. A finding that proposes one is
+    a validator error.
+    - Position rules still hold: an acronym leading a `lowerCamelCase` name is
+      down-cased as a unit (`urlSession`, `idToken` — never `URLSession` as a
+      property name).
     - Within a SINGLE declaration's own name, don't mix spellings of the same
       term (`tokenIdToEntryIDMap` is flaggable — pick one spelling inside one
       name). Consistency across different declarations, files, or with
       surrounding code is NOT required and NOT flaggable.
-    - This carve-out covers only the `Id`/`ID` pair. Other acronyms (`Url`,
-      `Json`, `Http`, `Eos`, `Uuid`, …) remain governed by the uniform-casing
-      rule above.
   ```

-- [ ] **Reconcile line 10** (the uniform-acronym bullet): its wrong-examples list reads `` (`Url`, `Json`, `Http`, `deviceId` are all wrong) `` — remove `deviceId` from the list (it is now an accepted spelling per the carve-out), leaving the other examples intact.
-- [ ] **Reconcile line 13** (the "wins over local file prevalence" tiebreaker): its example is the ID pair (`entryId` → flag toward `entryID`), which now contradicts the carve-out. Rewrite the example to a non-ID acronym (`deviceUrl` → flag toward `deviceURL`) and keep the tiebreaker's substance; the new bullet's Id/ID carve-out takes precedence over it.
-- [ ] **Add a content regression test** alongside the existing builtin-loading tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/builtin/mod.rs` (pattern: `test_test_integrity_homes_no_hard_code`, ~line 231): load builtins via `load_builtins` and assert the swift `casing` ruleset's loaded rule text (a) contains `BOTH accepted`, and (b) no longer contains the retired directive text `DON'T: \`entryId\`` nor `deviceId` in the acronym wrong-examples — so the carve-out can't be silently reverted or half-applied.
+- [ ] **Keep the canonical mixed-case bullet (line 14) and ordinary-word bullet (line 15) unchanged** — `LoRA`/`OAuth`/`GraphQL`/`gRPC`/`IPv6`/`macOS` keep their canonical spellings and are never flattened; `radarDetector` stays an ordinary word. Also unchanged: the type/member case bullets (lines 8–9) and the no-SCREAMING_SNAKE / no-Hungarian bullets (lines 16–17).
+- [ ] **Add a content regression test** alongside the existing builtin-loading tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/builtin/mod.rs` (pattern: `test_test_integrity_homes_no_hard_code`, ~line 231): load builtins via `load_builtins` and assert the swift `casing` ruleset's loaded rule text (a) contains `BOTH accepted`, and (b) no longer contains the retired directives — neither `are all wrong` applied to `Url`/`Json`, nor `DON'T: \`entryId\``, nor the "flag toward the uniform form" tiebreaker phrase — so the relaxation can't be silently reverted or half-applied.

-**Deploy note (not part of this card's code change):** `sah init` never deletes or refreshes already-deployed validator stores in consuming projects (known fossil behavior) — after this merges, deployed copies (e.g. in the mlx repo) need a redeploy/prune for the new rule to take effect there.
+**Deploy note (not part of this card's code change):** `sah init` never refreshes already-deployed validator stores in consuming projects (known fossil behavior) — after this merges, deployed copies (e.g. in the mlx repo) need a redeploy/prune for the relaxed rule to take effect there.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [ ] `builtin/validators/swift/rules/casing.md` contains the replacement bullet verbatim, and no longer contains `DON'T: \`entryId\`` anywhere.
-- [ ] Neither line 10's wrong-examples nor line 13's tiebreaker example references the `Id`/`ID` pair; the tiebreaker rule itself survives with a non-ID example.
-- [ ] The swift `casing` ruleset still loads cleanly through `load_builtins` (frontmatter intact — `name: casing` with its description).
-- [ ] The new regression test fails against the pre-change file content and passes after.
+- [ ] `builtin/validators/swift/rules/casing.md` presents both acronym spellings as accepted and states that a spelling-conversion rename is a validator error; the old uniform-only directives (`are all wrong` list with `Url`/`Json`, the `DON'T: \`entryId\`` bullet, the flag-toward-uniform tiebreaker) are gone.
+- [ ] The single-name no-mixing sub-rule (`tokenIdToEntryIDMap` flaggable) and the leading-position down-casing sub-rule are present in the new text.
+- [ ] The canonical mixed-case (`LoRA`, `OAuth`, …) and ordinary-word (`radarDetector`) bullets are byte-identical to before.
+- [ ] The swift `casing` ruleset still loads cleanly through `load_builtins` (frontmatter intact — `name: casing`); the new regression test fails on the pre-change content and passes after.

 ## Tests

-- [ ] New test in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/builtin/mod.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` asserting the loaded swift casing rule text contains `BOTH accepted` and contains neither `DON'T: \`entryId\`` nor a `deviceId` wrong-example.
+- [ ] New test in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/builtin/mod.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` asserting the loaded swift casing rule text contains `BOTH accepted` and contains none of: an `are all wrong` list naming `Url`/`Json`, `DON'T: \`entryId\``, or the flag-toward-uniform tiebreaker phrase.
 - [ ] Existing builtin loader tests (`test_focused_validators_have_clean_manifest_frontmatter` etc.) still pass — frontmatter untouched.
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-validators builtin` — green.

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,6 +1,11 @@
 ---
 assignees:
 - claude-code
+comments:

 ## What

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -16,7 +16,7 @@

 Edits to `builtin/validators/swift/rules/casing.md` (current line numbers):

-- [ ] **Rewrite the line-10 uniform-acronym bullet** (currently: "Acronyms and initialisms are cased uniformly … (`Url`, `Json`, `Http`, `deviceId` are all wrong)") and **delete the line-12 ID bullet and line-13 prevalence-tiebreaker bullet**, replacing all three with one flexible rule to this effect (wording may be polished, substance verbatim):
+- [x] **Rewrite the line-10 uniform-acronym bullet** (currently: "Acronyms and initialisms are cased uniformly … (`Url`, `Json`, `Http`, `deviceId` are all wrong)") and **delete the line-12 ID bullet and line-13 prevalence-tiebreaker bullet**, replacing all three with one flexible rule to this effect (wording may be polished, substance verbatim):

   ```
   - **Acronym spelling is flexible — the uniform form (`URL`, `ID`, `JSON`, `HTTP`)
@@ -38,23 +38,27 @@
       surrounding code is NOT required and NOT flaggable.
   ```

-- [ ] **Keep the canonical mixed-case bullet (line 14) and ordinary-word bullet (line 15) unchanged** — `LoRA`/`OAuth`/`GraphQL`/`gRPC`/`IPv6`/`macOS` keep their canonical spellings and are never flattened; `radarDetector` stays an ordinary word. Also unchanged: the type/member case bullets (lines 8–9) and the no-SCREAMING_SNAKE / no-Hungarian bullets (lines 16–17).
-- [ ] **Add a content regression test** alongside the existing builtin-loading tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/builtin/mod.rs` (pattern: `test_test_integrity_homes_no_hard_code`, ~line 231): load builtins via `load_builtins` and assert the swift `casing` ruleset's loaded rule text (a) contains `BOTH accepted`, and (b) no longer contains the retired directives — neither `are all wrong` applied to `Url`/`Json`, nor `DON'T: \`entryId\``, nor the "flag toward the uniform form" tiebreaker phrase — so the relaxation can't be silently reverted or half-applied.
+- [x] **Keep the canonical mixed-case bullet (line 14) and ordinary-word bullet (line 15) unchanged** — `LoRA`/`OAuth`/`GraphQL`/`gRPC`/`IPv6`/`macOS` keep their canonical spellings and are never flattened; `radarDetector` stays an ordinary word. Also unchanged: the type/member case bullets (lines 8–9) and the no-SCREAMING_SNAKE / no-Hungarian bullets (lines 16–17).
+- [x] **Add a content regression test** alongside the existing builtin-loading tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/builtin/mod.rs` (pattern: `test_test_integrity_homes_no_hard_code`, ~line 231): load builtins via `load_builtins` and assert the swift `casing` ruleset's loaded rule text (a) contains `BOTH accepted`, and (b) no longer contains the retired directives — neither `are all wrong` applied to `Url`/`Json`, nor `DON'T: \`entryId\``, nor the "flag toward the uniform form" tiebreaker phrase — so the relaxation can't be silently reverted or half-applied.

 **Deploy note (not part of this card's code change):** `sah init` never refreshes already-deployed validator stores in consuming projects (known fossil behavior) — after this merges, deployed copies (e.g. in the mlx repo) need a redeploy/prune for the relaxed rule to take effect there.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [ ] `builtin/validators/swift/rules/casing.md` presents both acronym spellings as accepted and states that a spelling-conversion rename is a validator error; the old uniform-only directives (`are all wrong` list with `Url`/`Json`, the `DON'T: \`entryId\`` bullet, the flag-toward-uniform tiebreaker) are gone.
-- [ ] The single-name no-mixing sub-rule (`tokenIdToEntryIDMap` flaggable) and the leading-position down-casing sub-rule are present in the new text.
-- [ ] The canonical mixed-case (`LoRA`, `OAuth`, …) and ordinary-word (`radarDetector`) bullets are byte-identical to before.
-- [ ] The swift `casing` ruleset still loads cleanly through `load_builtins` (frontmatter intact — `name: casing`); the new regression test fails on the pre-change content and passes after.
+- [x] `builtin/validators/swift/rules/casing.md` presents both acronym spellings as accepted and states that a spelling-conversion rename is a validator error; the old uniform-only directives (`are all wrong` list with `Url`/`Json`, the `DON'T: \`entryId\`` bullet, the flag-toward-uniform tiebreaker) are gone.
+- [x] The single-name no-mixing sub-rule (`tokenIdToEntryIDMap` flaggable) and the leading-position down-casing sub-rule are present in the new text.
+- [x] The canonical mixed-case (`LoRA`, `OAuth`, …) and ordinary-word (`radarDetector`) bullets are byte-identical to before.
+- [x] The swift `casing` ruleset still loads cleanly through `load_builtins` (frontmatter intact — `name: casing`); the new regression test fails on the pre-change content and passes after.

 ## Tests

-- [ ] New test in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/builtin/mod.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` asserting the loaded swift casing rule text contains `BOTH accepted` and contains none of: an `are all wrong` list naming `Url`/`Json`, `DON'T: \`entryId\``, or the flag-toward-uniform tiebreaker phrase.
-- [ ] Existing builtin loader tests (`test_focused_validators_have_clean_manifest_frontmatter` etc.) still pass — frontmatter untouched.
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-validators builtin` — green.
+- [x] New test in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/builtin/mod.rs` `#[cfg(test)]` asserting the loaded swift casing rule text contains `BOTH accepted` and contains none of: an `are all wrong` list naming `Url`/`Json`, `DON'T: \`entryId\``, or the flag-toward-uniform tiebreaker phrase.
+- [x] Existing builtin loader tests (`test_focused_validators_have_clean_manifest_frontmatter` etc.) still pass — frontmatter untouched.
+- [x] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-validators builtin` — green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
